have h₁ : Continuous g'' := by
      have h₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Show that each term in the double sum is continuous and hence the sum is continuous
        have h₃ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y))) := by
          intro i j
          have h₄ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := by
            apply Continuous.add
            · -- Continuity of (1 - t) • x
              apply Continuous.smul
              · exact continuous_const.sub continuous_id
              · exact continuous_const
            · -- Continuity of t • y
              apply Continuous.smul
              · exact continuous_id
              · exact continuous_const
          have h₅ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) : E n → ℝ) ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y)) := by
            -- Since f is C², the mixed partial derivatives are continuous
            have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
              have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
              have h₈ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
                apply h₇.contDiff_nth_partial
                <;> simp_all [Finset.card_univ]
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              have h₉ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
                apply h₈.contDiff_nth_partial
                <;> simp_all [Finset.card_univ]
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              exact h₉
            have h₇ : Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := h₆.continuous
            have h₈ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y : ℝ → E n) := h₄
            exact h₇.comp h₈
          -- Continuity of the product (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))
          have h₉ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) : ℝ → ℝ) := continuous_const
          have h₁₀ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j) : ℝ → ℝ) := continuous_const
          have h₁₁ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) : ℝ → ℝ) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₉ h₁₀
          have h₁₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y)) : ℝ → ℝ) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₁₁ h₅
          exact h₁₂
        -- Double sum of continuous functions is continuous
        have h₆ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y))) := by
          apply Continuous.sum
          intro i _
          apply Continuous.sum
          intro j _
          exact h₃ i j
        exact h₆
      -- Since g'' is defined as this sum, it is continuous
      dsimp only [interpolated_deriv2] at *
      exact h₂
    exact h₁

have h₁ : Continuous g'' := by
      -- Prove that each term in the double sum is continuous
      have h₂ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
        intro i j
        have h₃ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
        have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
          have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
            apply ContDiff.nth_partial
            <;> norm_num
            <;> linarith
          exact h₆
        have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
          have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := h₄
          have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            apply contDiff_const
          exact h₇
        have h₆ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
          have h₇ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ((1 - t) • x + t • y : E n) := by
            continuity
          have h₈ : Continuous (fun p : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) p : ℝ)) := by
            have h₉ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := h₅
            have h₁₀ : Continuous (fun p : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) p : ℝ)) := h₉.continuous
            exact h₁₀
          have h₉ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
            exact h₈.comp h₇
          exact h₉
        have h₇ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
          have h₈ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) := by continuity
          have h₉ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j : ℝ) := by continuity
          have h₁₀ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := h₆
          have h₁₁ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₈ h₉
          have h₁₂ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₁₁ h₁₀
          exact h₁₂
        exact h₇
      -- Sum of continuous functions is continuous
      have h₃ : Continuous g'' := by
        have h₄ : g'' = fun t => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
          rfl
        rw [h₄]
        -- Use the fact that the sum of continuous functions is continuous
        have h₅ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
          -- Use the fact that each term is continuous and the sum is finite
          have h₆ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
            intro i
            have h₇ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
              apply Continuous.finset_sum
              intro j _
              exact h₂ i j
            exact h₇
          have h₈ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) : ℝ) := by
            apply Continuous.finset_sum
            intro i _
            exact h₆ i
          exact h₈
        exact h₅
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  sorry

But this is perhaps not yet formulated in Lean as a direct lemma.

Alternatively, perhaps we can use that `ContDiff ℝ 2 f` implies that all second mixed partials exist and are continuous. Therefore, `nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)` is continuous.

But perhaps it is not needed because:

      have h4 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
        -- Prove that the interpolator function is continuous

        · apply Continuous.smul_const
          apply Continuous.sub
          · exact continuous_const
          · exact continuous_id
        · apply Continuous.smul_const

      have h5 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Use the fact that each term is continuous and the sum is continuous
        have h6 : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          intro i j
          have h7 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := h4
          have h8 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
            -- Prove that the composition is continuous
            have h9 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h10 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial i f) := by
              -- Prove that the first partial is smooth
              have h11 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial i f) := by
                apply ContDiff.nth_partial
                <;> simp_all [Singleton.mem_insert, Singleton.mem_singleton]
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              exact h11
            have h12 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial j (nth_partial i f)) := by
              -- Prove that the second partial is smooth
              have h13 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial j (nth_partial i f)) := by
                apply ContDiff.nth_partial
                <;> simp_all [Singleton.mem_insert, Singleton.mem_singleton]
                <;> norm_num
                <;> linarith
              exact h13
            have h14 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) z) := by
              -- Prove that the second partial is differentiable
              have h15 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) z) := by
                have h16 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
                  apply ContDiff.nth_partial
                  <;> simp_all [Singleton.mem_insert, Singleton.mem_singleton]
                  <;> norm_num
                  <;> linarith
                exact h16.of_le (by norm_num)
              exact h15
            have h17 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
              -- Prove that the composition is continuous
              have h18 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := h4
              have h19 : Continuous (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) z) := by
                -- Prove that the second partial is continuous
                have h20 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by

                  <;> simp_all [Singleton.mem_insert, Singleton.mem_singleton]
                  <;> norm_num
                  <;> linarith
                exact h20.continuous
              exact h19.comp h18
            exact h17

          -- The composition of a continuous function with a continuous function is continuous
          have h₆ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ((1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y : E n)) := h₃
          have h₇ : Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            -- The second partial derivative is continuous because it is C^1
            apply h₄.continuous
          exact h₇.comp h₆
        -- Prove that the product of continuous functions is continuous
        have h₆ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          have h₇ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ)) := continuous_const
          have h₈ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j : ℝ)) := continuous_const
          have h₉ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := h₅
          have h₁₀ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ)) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₇ h₈
          have h₁₁ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₁₀ h₉
          exact h₁₁
        exact h₆
      -- Prove that the double sum of continuous functions is continuous
      have h₃ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        apply Continuous.sum
        intro i _
        apply Continuous.sum
        intro j _
        exact h₂ i j
      exact h₃
    -- The function g'' is defined as the double sum above, so it is continuous
    simpa [g''] using h₁
  exact by
    simp_all [g₀, g, g', g'']
    <;>
    try norm_num
    <;>
    try linarith
    <;>
    try ring_nf
    <;>
    try field_simp
    <;>
    try nlinarith
    <;>
    try simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, abs_of_nonpos]
    <;>
    try linarith

intro j _
    exact h₁ i j
  
  exact h₂

have h2 : g'' = (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t))) := rfl
    rw [h2]
    apply Continuous.sum
    intro i _
    apply Continuous.sum
    intro j _
    have h3 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t))) := by
      have h4 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => interpolator x y t) := by
        have h5 : Differentiable ℝ (interpolator x y) := g₀_diff
        exact h5.continuous
      have h6 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t)) := by
        have h7 : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
          have h8 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h9 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
            exact contDiff_id.nth_partial j |>.comp (h8.nth_partial) _
          have h10 : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            exact contDiff_id.nth_partial i |>.comp (h9.nth_partial) _
          exact h10
        have h11 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t)) := by
          have h12 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => interpolator x y t) := g₀_diff.continuous
          exact h7.continuous.continuousAt |>.comp h12
        exact h11
      have h13 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t))) := by
        have h14 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j)) := by
          have h15 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i)) := continuous_const
          have h16 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j)) := continuous_const
          have h17 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j)) := by
            apply Continuous.mul
            · exact h15
            · exact h16
          exact h17
        have h18 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t))) := h6
        have h19 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) (interpolator x y t))) := by
          apply Continuous.mul
          · exact h14
          · exact h18
        exact h19
      exact h13
    exact h3
  sorry

have h : Continuous g'' := by
      -- Prove that g'' is continuous using the continuity of f and its partial derivatives
      have h₁ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
      have h₂ : Continuous f := by
        exact h₁.continuous
      have h₃ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Continuous (nth_partial i f) := by
        intro i
        have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
        have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
          apply ContDiff.nth_partial
          <;> simp_all [h₄]
          <;> norm_num
          <;> linarith
        exact h₅.continuous
      have h₄ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
        intro i j
        have h₅ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
        have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
          have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h₈ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
            apply ContDiff.nth_partial
            <;> simp_all [h₇]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> linarith
          have h₉ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            apply ContDiff.nth_partial
            <;> simp_all [h₈]
            <;> norm_num
            <;> linarith
          exact h₉
        exact h₆.continuous
      -- Use the continuity of the partial derivatives to prove the continuity of g''
      have h₅ : Continuous g'' := by
        -- g'' is a sum of products of continuous functions, hence continuous
        have h₆ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
          apply Continuous.tsum
          intro i
          apply Continuous.tsum
          intro j
          have h₇ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
            have h₈ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) := by continuity
            have h₉ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (t : ℝ) := by continuity
            have h₁₀ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => ((1 - t) • x + t • y : E n) := by
              continuity
            have h₁₁ : Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := h₄ i j
            have h₁₂ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
              apply Continuous.comp (h₄ i j) h₁₀
            have h₁₃ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) := by continuity
            have h₁₄ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j : ℝ) := by continuity
            have h₁₅ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) := by
              continuity
            have h₁₆ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
              continuity
            exact h₁₆
          exact h₇
        simpa [g''] using h₆
      exact h₅
    exact h
  sorry

exact (fun_prop)
  sorry

simp only [interpolated_deriv2]
    apply Continuous.sum
    intro i _
    apply Continuous.sum
    intro j _
    have h₁ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      have h₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j)) := by continuity
      have h₃ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        have h₄ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := by
          exact (fc.contDiffAt.le (by norm_num)).nth_partial 1 _ |>.contDiffStrictOn
        have h₅ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - (1 : ℝ) * t) • x + t • y) := by continuity
        exact h₄.continuous.differentiableOn.hasContinuousDerivAt.continuous.comp h₅
      exact Continuous.mul (Continuous.mul h₂ h₃) (continuous_const)
    exact h₁

have hc : Continuous g'' := by
      -- Prove that `g''` is continuous using the fact that sums and products of continuous functions are continuous.
      have h1 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Prove continuity of each term in the double sum
        have h2 : Continuous (fun (t : ℝ) => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
          -- The function `t ↦ (1 - t)x + t y` is continuous because it is a linear combination of continuous functions.
          apply Continuous.add
          · apply Continuous.smul
            · exact continuous_const.sub continuous_id
            · exact continuous_const
          · apply Continuous.smul
            · exact continuous_id
            · exact continuous_const
        -- The `nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)` is C^0 because `f` is C^2.
        have h3 : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          intro i j
          have h4 : Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            -- The second partial derivatives of `f` are continuous since `f` is C^2.
            have h5 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h6 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := by
              -- First partial derivatives are C^1.
              have h7 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
              exact contDiff_id.nth_partial i |>.comp h7 (by norm_num)
            -- Second partial derivatives are continuous.
            have h8 : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
              have h9 : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial i f) := h6
              exact contDiff_id.nth_partial j |>.comp h9 (by norm_num) |>.contDiffOn
              <;> norm_num
            exact h8.continuous
          have h10 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := h2
          exact h4.comp h10
        -- The products of continuous functions are continuous.
        have h11 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
          have h12 : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
            intro i j
            have h13 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := h3 i j
            have h14 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j)) := by
              apply Continuous.mul
              · exact continuous_const
              · exact continuous_const
            exact Continuous.mul h14 h13
          -- Sum of continuous functions is continuous.
          have h15 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ j : Fin n, (y (⟨0, by
              have h : n ≠ 0 := by
                by_contra hn
                simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, E]
                <;> aesop
              omega⟩ : Fin n) : Fin n) - x (⟨0, by
              have h : n ≠ 0 := by
                by_contra hn
                simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, E]
                <;> aesop
              omega⟩ : Fin n)) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial (⟨0, by
              have h : n ≠ 0 := by
                by_contra hn
                simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, E]
                <;> aesop
              omega⟩ : Fin n) (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by sorry
          sorry
        exact h11
      sorry
    exact hc
`nth_partial j f` is equal

have h1 : g'' = fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
      rfl
    rw [h1]
    -- Use the fact that the sum of continuous functions is continuous
    have h2 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
      apply Continuous.finset_sum
      intro i _
      apply Continuous.finset_sum
      intro j _
      -- Each term in the sum is continuous
      have h3 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ)) := continuous_const
      have h4 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j : ℝ)) := continuous_const
      have h5 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        -- The function (1 - t) • x + t • y is continuous
        have h6 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y) := by
          -- The function (1 - t) • x + t • y is continuous
          have h7 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x) := by
            -- The function (1 - t) • x is continuous
            apply Continuous.smul
            · exact continuous_const.sub continuous_id
            · exact continuous_const
          have h8 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (t : ℝ) • y) := by
            -- The function t • y is continuous
            apply Continuous.smul
            · exact continuous_id
            · exact continuous_const
          -- The sum of continuous functions is continuous
          apply Continuous.add h7 h8
        -- The composition of continuous functions is continuous
        have h9 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          -- The function nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) is continuous
          have h10 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
          have h11 : Continuous (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            apply C1.continuous
            apply Differentiable.nth_partial
            <;>
            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try simp_all [ContDiff.differentiable]) <;>
            (try apply Differentiable.nth_partial) <;>
            (try simp_all [ContDiff.differentiable]) <;>
            (try norm_num)
            <;>
            (try
              {
                have h12 : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
                have h13 : ContDiff ℝ 2 (nth_partial j f) := by
                  apply ContDiff.nth_partial
                  <;> simp_all [ContDiff.differentiable]
                  <;> norm_num
                simp_all [ContDiff.differentiable]
              })
          -- The composition of continuous functions is continuous
          exact h11.comp h6
        exact h9
      -- The product of continuous functions is continuous
      have h6 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
        have h7 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j)) := by
          apply Continuous.mul h3 h4
        have h8 : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := h5
        apply Continuous.mul h7 h8
      exact h6
    exact h2
  sorry

-- Prove that g'' is continuous
    have h : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
    have h₁ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
      -- Prove that the interpolation function is continuous
      have h₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := by
        -- Use the fact that linear functions are continuous
        continuity
      exact h₂
    -- Use the fact that the composition of continuous functions is continuous
    have h₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
      -- Prove that the sum of continuous functions is continuous
      have h₃ : ∀ (i : Fin n) (j : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        intro i j
        -- Prove that each term in the sum is continuous
        have h₄ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := h₁
        have h₅ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f))((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          -- Use the fact that f is twice continuously differentiable
          have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := by
            -- Prove that the second partial derivative is continuous
            have h₇ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h₈ : ContDiff ℝ 1 (nth_partial j f) := h₇.nth_partial 1 j
            have h₉ : ContDiff ℝ 0 (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f)) := h₈.nth_partial 0 i
            exact h₉
          have h₈ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) := h₁
          -- Use the fact that the composition of continuous functions is continuous
          have h₉ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f))((1 - t) • x + t • y)) :=
            (h₆.continuous).comp h₈
          exact h₉
        -- Multiply by the constants (y i - x i) and (y j - x j)
        have h₁₀ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
          have h₁₁ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j)) := continuous_const
          have h₁₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f))((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := h₅
          -- Use the fact that the product of continuous functions is continuous
          have h₁₃ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) :=
            Continuous.mul h₁₁ h₁₂
          exact h₁₃
        exact h₁₀
      -- Sum over i and j
      have h₄ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        have h₅ : ∀ (i : Fin n), Continuous (fun t : ℝ => ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
          intro i
          apply Continuous.sum
          intro j _
          exact h₃ i j
        apply Continuous.sum
        intro i _
        exact h₅ i
      exact h₄
    -- Conclude that g'' is continuous
    have h₃ : g'' = (fun t : ℝ => ∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    exact h₂

have h₁ : Continuous g'' := by
      -- Prove that g'' is continuous by showing each term in the double sum is continuous.
      have h₂ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (∑ i : Fin n, ∑ j : Fin n, (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y))) := by
        -- Use the fact that each term is continuous and the sum of continuous functions is continuous.
        apply Continuous.sum
        intro i _
        apply Continuous.sum
        intro j _
        -- Each term (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) is continuous.
        have h₃ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i) * (y j - x j) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
          -- (y i - x i) and (y j - x j) are constants, and nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) is continuous.
          have h₄ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (1 - t : ℝ) • x + t • y := by
            -- The function t ↦ (1 - t) • x + t • y is continuous.
            continuity
          have h₅ : Continuous fun z : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) z : ℝ) := by
            -- The partial derivatives are continuous because f is C².
            have h₆ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
            have h₇ : Differentiable ℝ f := f_diff
            -- The nth partial derivatives are continuous because f is C².
            have h₈ : Continuous (fun z : E n => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) z : ℝ)) := by
              sorry
            exact h₈
          -- Compose the continuous functions to get the continuity of the term.
          have h₉ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
            continuity
          have h₁₀ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) := by continuity
          have h₁₁ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y j - x j : ℝ) := by continuity
          have h₁₂ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) := by continuity
          -- Multiply the continuous functions to get the final continuity.
          have h₁₃ : Continuous fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i : ℝ) * (y j - x j : ℝ) * (nth_partial i (nth_partial j f) ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) := by
            apply Continuous.mul h₁₂ h₉
          exact h₁₃
        exact h₃
      -- The sum of continuous functions is continuous.
      exact h₂
    exact h₁
  sorry

have h₁ : g'' = fun t => interpolated_deriv2 x y f t := rfl
    rw [h₁]
    -- Prove that the second derivative is continuous
    -- Use the fact that the second partial derivatives are continuous
    -- to show that the second derivative is continuous
    have h₂ : Continuous (fun t : ℝ => interpolated_deriv2 x y f t) := by
      -- The second derivative is a sum of products of continuous functions
      -- because the second partial derivatives are continuous
      have h₃ : ContDiff ℝ 2 f := fc
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ f := fc.differentiable (by norm_num)
      -- Use the fact that the second partial derivatives are continuous to show continuity
      -- of the second derivative
      sorry
    exact h₂
  have h₁ : |f x - f y| ≤ ε * ∑ i : Fin n, |nth_partial i f x| + (n ^ 2 / 2 : ℝ) * ε ^ 2 := by sorry
  exact h₁
